hypothesis: Reflect padding in ConvNeXt baseline will reduce inward boundary reflections versus zero padding.
Replicate padding in ConvNeXt baseline will stabilize edge amplitudes better than zero padding.
Circular padding in ConvNeXt baseline will reduce hard-edge reflection artifacts.
Standard conv blocks with reflect padding will improve boundary smoothness relative to ConvNeXt.
Depthwise-separable blocks with reflect padding will reduce boundary noise while keeping rollout skill.
Kernel size 7 with reflect padding will improve boundary-context capture and reduce reflected wave build-up.
Kernel size 3 with reflect padding will reduce oversmoothing and lower edge amplification.
Disabling dilation cycling dilation_cycle=1 under reflect padding will reduce edge gridding artifacts.
Mild dilation_cycle=2 under reflect padding will balance receptive field growth and boundary stability.
Depthwise-separable plus kernel 7 under reflect padding will provide the strongest boundary artifact suppression.
